question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `NodeMap` is a `map[string]hiero.AccountID` with no validation of the IP:port key format to inject a node entry with an empty string key, causing undefined behavior in node selection and potentially routing fees to an unintended account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `mergeExternalConfigFile()` function silently ignoring missing config files to cause the rosetta node to run with only default configuration values, where default fee parameters may be outside the design parameters for the target network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `HIERO_MIRROR_ROSETTA_API_CONFIG` environment variable to point to a world-readable config file that has been modified to contain incorrect fee-related node account IDs, causing the rosetta node to route fees to attacker-controlled accounts?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `GetDsn()` method's inclusion of `sslmode=disable` to perform a network-level interception of fee schedule data transmitted between the rosetta node and the database, modifying fee values in transit to be outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Cache] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Cache.MaxSize` of zero for the `EntityCacheKey` cache to disable entity caching entirely, causing every fee-related entity lookup to hit the database and enabling timing attacks that exploit database query latency to infer fee schedule contents?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.Network` field being set to an empty string to cause `hiero.ClientForName()` to fail or return a default client, resulting in the rosetta node using an unintended network's fee schedule and reporting fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeMapDecodeHookFunc` not validating that node account IDs are within the expected shard/realm to inject a node account ID from a different shard (e.g., `1.0.3` instead of `0.0.3`), causing fee payments to be directed to a non-existent or attacker-controlled cross-shard account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Http] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Http.ReadTimeout` of zero (no timeout) to hold open connections to the `/construction/metadata` endpoint indefinitely, exhausting server goroutines and preventing legitimate fee metadata requests from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the viper key delimiter `::` used in `LoadConfig()` to inject configuration keys containing `::` that override nested fee-related configuration values, causing fee parameters to be set to attacker-controlled values?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Pool] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a `Pool.MaxIdleConnections` of zero to cause the database driver to close all idle connections immediately after use, creating connection churn that causes fee schedule queries to fail under load and the node to return default fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.NodeRefreshInterval` being a `time.Duration` with no maximum validation to supply a duration of `math.MaxInt64` nanoseconds, effectively disabling node refresh and causing the node to use a stale `NodeMap` with incorrect fee-routing account IDs indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Db] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Db.Pool.MaxOpenConnections` being set to a very high value to cause the rosetta node to open thousands of database connections simultaneously, exhausting database server resources and causing fee schedule queries to fail, resulting in default fees being used?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: Config] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Config.Online` field being `true` combined with an empty `Config.Nodes` map to cause the rosetta node to use `hiero.ClientForName()` with a network that has a different fee schedule than expected, resulting in `GetDefaultMaxTransactionFee()` returning fees outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Struct: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `NodeMap` is a `map[string]hiero.AccountID` with no validation of the IP:port key format to inject a node entry with an empty string key, causing undefined behavior in node selection and potentially routing fees to an unintended account?
